equipment.name: Blade of Ad Hominem
equipment.description: A chipped shortsword that strikes at the person, not the argument. Its edge finds weakness in character rather than armour.

equipment.name: Staff of Infinite Regress
equipment.description: A gnarled oak staff carved with turtles standing on turtles. Each impact echoes backward through an endless chain of justification.

equipment.name: Hammer of Brute Force
equipment.description: A crude iron maul that makes no pretense of elegance. When argument fails, volume and mass rarely do.

equipment.name: Quill of the Dialectician
equipment.description: A silver-nibbed pen that writes counterarguments in the air. Each stroke of thesis summons antithesis — synthesis is left to the wounded.

equipment.name: Dagger of Occam
equipment.description: The simplest explanation is usually correct. The simplest weapon is usually sharp. This one is both.

equipment.name: Helm of Confirmation Bias
equipment.description: A visored helm that filters out contradictory evidence. You see only what supports your worldview — and fight accordingly.

equipment.name: Crown of the Philosopher King
equipment.description: A simple circlet of bronze laurels worn by those who rule through reason. Sharpens perception at the cost of raw conviction.

equipment.name: Hood of Fermi's Absence
equipment.description: A dark hood that makes you paradoxically undetectable. You should be visible — yet observers report nothing.

equipment.name: Breastplate of Theseus
equipment.description: Every dent has been hammered out, every plate replaced. Is it the same armour? The question is academic — it still stops swords.

equipment.name: Robes of the Sophist
equipment.description: Flowing silk garments that shimmer with rhetorical authority. They protect not through thickness but through the sheer weight of persuasion.

equipment.name: Chainmail of Logical Necessity
equipment.description: Each link is forged from a valid syllogism. The armour holds because its structure is logically necessary — it cannot not protect.

equipment.name: Gauntlets of the Straw Man
equipment.description: Iron-plated gloves stuffed with straw. They strike a weaker version of the target's defenses, making every blow land a little easier.

equipment.name: Gloves of Slippery Slope
equipment.description: Silk-lined gloves that make every grab lead inevitably to the next. Once you start, the cascade of consequence is unstoppable.

equipment.name: Boots of Achilles' Speed
equipment.description: Winged sandals that let you close any distance — though like Achilles and the tortoise, you may never quite catch your quarry.

equipment.name: Greaves of the Unmoved Mover
equipment.description: Heavy iron greaves that root you in place with divine certainty. You move others; nothing moves you.

equipment.name: Ring of Circular Reasoning
equipment.description: This ring is powerful because it is magical. It is magical because it is powerful. The logic is flawed; the enchantment holds.

equipment.name: Amulet of the False Dilemma
equipment.description: A black-and-white gemstone pendant. It presents only two options to your enemies: lose quickly, or lose slowly.

equipment.name: Pendant of Survivorship Bias
equipment.description: Every previous owner of this amulet survived their battles — or so the records claim. Those who didn't left no records.

equipment.name: Brooch of the Gambler's Fallacy
equipment.description: After a streak of misfortune, good luck is surely due. The brooch's magic turns this fallacy into prophecy — sometimes.

equipment.name: Mace of Appeal to Authority
equipment.description: A heavy ceremonial mace stamped with the seals of a hundred institutions. Its authority is borrowed, but the concussions are original.

equipment.name: Jerkin of the Red Herring
equipment.description: A leather vest dyed distracting scarlet. Enemies fixate on it, losing track of your actual vulnerable spots.

equipment.name: Mask of the Tu Quoque
equipment.description: A mirrored half-mask that reflects accusations back at the accuser. 'You do it too' has never been so literal.

equipment.name: Sash of the Sunk Cost
equipment.description: A tattered battle-sash from a war that should have ended long ago. You've invested too much to stop now — and the sash agrees.

equipment.name: Vambraces of No True Scotsman
equipment.description: Arm guards that redefine 'hit' after each strike lands. No true blow could have breached them — therefore it didn't.
